roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 2 milestones: beginner with 5 hrs/week and an extreme gap from current identity to Data Scientist, requiring a focused, foundational path within the 64-hour budget.

vision_profile.current_state: A motivated individual with no prior professional experience in data science or related fields, seeking to enter the AI industry.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Lack of foundational technical skills, practical project experience, and understanding of the data science workflow.
vision_profile.top_motivation: To secure an entry-level position as a Data Scientist or Data Analyst and build a rewarding career in AI, leveraging data to drive insights and innovation.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: The learner can confidently manipulate, clean, and visualize data using Python, demonstrating foundational data analysis skills for entry-level roles or internships.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 2 modules because this milestone focuses on core programming and data handling, requiring distinct clusters for language fundamentals and practical data manipulation/visualization to establish a solid base.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a meticulous Data Foundations Associate, building the essential skills to understand and interact with data, preparing you for your first practical experiences.

project.title: Exploratory Data Analysis for a Public Dataset
project.description: Analyze a publicly available dataset (e.g., Kaggle dataset on housing prices or sales data) to uncover trends, patterns, and anomalies. Clean the data, perform exploratory analysis, and create compelling visualizations.
project.deliverable: A Jupyter Notebook with documented code, analysis, visualizations, and a brief summary report of key findings, ready for presentation.
seeded_errors: The AI-generated data cleaning script introduces NaN values in critical columns due to incorrect regex patterns, requiring the learner to debug and correct the data imputation logic.

modules.title: Python for Data Handling
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills to cover essential Python programming constructs and the fundamental libraries for data manipulation, crucial for any data role.

skills.title: Python Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: Python is the lingua franca of data science; mastering its basics is non-negotiable for writing efficient and readable data scripts.

scenario.title: Debugging a Syntax Error in a Data Script

skills.title: Data Structures
skills.why_this_skill: Efficiently storing and accessing data is fundamental; understanding lists, dictionaries, and sets optimizes data manipulation tasks.

scenario.title: Optimizing Data Storage for Performance

skills.title: NumPy
skills.why_this_skill: NumPy is the backbone for numerical operations in Python, essential for handling arrays and matrices efficiently in data science and machine learning.

scenario.title: Vectorizing a Slow Loop with NumPy

skills.title: Pandas
skills.why_this_skill: Pandas DataFrames are the primary tool for tabular data manipulation, essential for loading, cleaning, and transforming real-world datasets.

scenario.title: Cleaning Messy Customer Data with Pandas
Aggregating Sales Data for Regional Performance Analysis

science.desc: A critical data ingestion pipeline fails due to unexpected data types in the source, requiring you to debug the Pandas loading script and implement robust error handling.

modules.title: Data Analysis & Visualization
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills to cover the complete cycle of exploratory data analysis, statistical understanding, and effective visual communication of insights.

scenario.title: Identifying Outliers in Customer Spending Data

skills.title: Matplotlib & Seaborn for Visualization
skills.why_this_skill: Effective data visualization is key to communicating insights; Matplotlib and Seaborn are standard tools for creating publication-quality plots.

video.title: Data Visualization with Matplotlib and Seaborn

scenario.title: Creating an Effective Dashboard for Business Stakeholders

skills.title: Basic Statistics for Data Analysis
skills.why_this_skill: A solid grasp of statistics is crucial for interpreting data, understanding distributions, and making informed decisions based on evidence.

scenario.title: Interpreting A/B Test Results for a Marketing Campaign
Refining a Presentation for Executive Review

skills.title: SQL for Data Retrieval
skills.why_this_skill: SQL is indispensable for querying and retrieving data from databases, a fundamental skill for any data professional.

scenario.title: Extracting Specific Customer Segments from a Database

science.desc: A stakeholder complains that a key visualization is misleading due to incorrect data aggregation, requiring you to identify the error and refine the plot for clarity and accuracy.
Describe your process for exploring a new dataset. What steps do you take, and what tools do you use to uncover initial insights and potential issues?

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: The learner can build, evaluate, and prepare basic machine learning models for deployment to solve business problems, effectively communicating predictive insights to stakeholders.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 2 modules to cover the transition from data analysis to foundational machine learning, focusing on core ML concepts and practical application with model deployment considerations.
milestones.identity_statement: You are evolving into a Junior Data Analyst, capable of applying predictive models and extracting actionable insights, ready to contribute to data-driven decision-making.

project.title: Customer Churn Prediction Model
project.description: Develop a machine learning model to predict customer churn for a subscription service. Preprocess data, train a classification model, evaluate its performance, and prepare it for potential deployment.
project.deliverable: A Jupyter Notebook with the complete ML pipeline (data preprocessing, model training, evaluation), a model inference script, and a presentation summarizing findings and model limitations.
seeded_errors: The AI-assisted feature engineering step introduces severe data leakage, causing the model to show unrealistically high performance on the test set. The learner must identify and rectify the leakage.

modules.title: Introduction to Machine Learning
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills to establish a strong theoretical and practical foundation in core machine learning concepts and common algorithms.

scenario.title: Choosing the Right ML Task for a Business Problem
Predicting Housing Prices with Linear Regression
Building a Customer Churn Predictor
Selecting the Best Metric for a Fraud Detection Model

skills.title: Scikit-learn Pipelines
skills.why_this_skill: Scikit-learn is the most popular Python library for traditional machine learning, providing a unified interface for various algorithms and tools.

scenario.title: Building an End-to-End ML Pipeline with Scikit-learn

science.desc: A newly trained classification model performs poorly on unseen data, showing signs of overfitting. You need to identify the cause and apply regularization techniques to improve generalization.

modules.title: Applied Data Modeling & Deployment Basics
modules.skill_count_rationale: 5 skills to bridge the gap from theoretical ML to practical application, including advanced preprocessing, model tuning, and basic deployment considerations.

skills.title: Feature Engineering & Pipelines
skills.why_this_skill: Creating effective features from raw data is often the most impactful step in improving model performance and is a hallmark of skilled data scientists.

scenario.title: Improving Model Performance with Custom Features
Optimizing a Classification Model for High Recall
Analyzing Customer Reviews for Product Feedback
Deploying a Simple ML Model as a Web Service
Mitigating Bias in a Loan Approval Prediction Model

science.desc: A deployed model for personalized recommendations starts generating irrelevant suggestions for a specific user segment. You need to investigate potential data drift or model bias and propose a solution.
You've built a predictive model. How do you explain its predictions and limitations to a non-technical business stakeholder, and what steps would you take to ensure its ethical use?
